hero.title: Empowering Communities Through Technology & Education
hero.tagline: Project Sankalp is a national initiative dedicated to bridging the digital divide, mentoring future leaders, and fostering social innovation through sustainable community projects.

about.title: Who We Are
about.subtitle: Catalyzing social change through tech enablement and skill development.
about.description: Founded in 2021, Project Sankalp has grown from a local mentoring circle into a nationwide network of professionals, student mentors, and social innovators. We believe that technology education and leadership development should be accessible to all, regardless of socio-economic background. By setting up community labs and conducting bootcamps, we are unlocking the digital potential of thousands of individuals.
about.mission: Our mission is to democratize technical knowledge, build sustainable digital infrastructure, and foster local leadership to solve pressing community problems.
about.vision: A self-sustaining, digitally-inclusive society where every individual has the skills and resources to design their own future.

values.title: Inclusivity First
values.description: We build pathways for underrepresented communities, ensuring nobody is left behind in the digital age.

values.title: Sustainable Impact
values.description: We focus on long-term infrastructure, mentorship loops, and self-sustaining community structures.

values.title: Radical Empathy
values.description: Our programs are co-designed directly with local communities to solve their actual, felt needs.

values.title: Collaborative Innovation
values.description: We bring together academia, industry leaders, and grassroots advocates to drive holistic change.

history.title: The Spark
history.description: Project Sankalp begins as a peer-to-peer tech mentorship group in response to remote learning challenges, serving 50 children.

history.title: Community Labs Initiative
history.description: Launched our first 3 physical Community Digital Labs, equipping them with donated systems and high-speed internet.

history.title: Nationwide Expansion
history.description: Expanded to 12 cities, partner with 5 universities, and launch the Technical Leadership Cohort.

history.title: Institutional Recognition
history.description: Registered as an independent non-profit, recognized by national social impact councils for our digital literacy programs.

history.title: Scaling the Mission
history.description: Aiming to establish 50 labs and empower 20,000+ citizens through advanced coding, design, and digital literacy curricula.

impact.title: Our Impact in Numbers
impact.subtitle: Real numbers driving measurable progress across communities.

caseStudies.title: Bridges to Byte: Digital Literacy in Nari Village
caseStudies.challenge: Lack of internet and digital equipment left over 300 youths in Nari village isolated from modern education systems during secondary schooling.
caseStudies.solution: Project Sankalp established a community digital lab equipped with 10 solar-powered computer terminals and trained 2 local graduates to run daily literacy classes.
caseStudies.result: Within 12 months, 180+ students graduated, 14 secure tech-based remote jobs, and the local secondary school integrated computer science into their core curriculum.

caseStudies.title: Krishi-Tech: Empowing Rural Farmers with IoT Data
caseStudies.challenge: Frequent crop failures due to unpredictable water table fluctuations and lack of affordable soil moisture monitoring tools.
caseStudies.solution: Collaborated with tech university students to build low-cost IoT soil monitors. Conducted hands-on tech workshops for 40 local farming families to help them read dashboard metrics.
caseStudies.result: Reduced water waste by 35%, increased overall crop yield by 20% in the first harvest, and trained 5 youth technicians for device maintenance.

programs.title: Ongoing Programs & Initiatives
programs.subtitle: Designed to empower, educate, and build long-term digital independence.

list.title: Sankalp Digital Literacy Academy
list.description: Bringing fundamental computer science skills, internet navigation, and safety training to school children and adults in remote regions.

list.title: Tech Mentorship & Career Accelerator
list.description: Connecting industry software engineers and designers with underprivileged college students for structured 1-on-1 mentorship, resume reviews, and portfolio development.

list.title: Sankalp Smart Lab Setup
list.description: Sourcing donated hardware, setting up physical computer labs in community centers, and supplying solar setups for continuous access.

list.title: Community Social Tech Fellows
list.description: A 6-month stipend-backed fellowship for young developers to build open-source local solutions, including tracking health diagnostics and local business marketplaces.

team.title: Meet the Team
team.subtitle: The passionate innovators, mentors, and advocates behind Project Sankalp.

members.name: Dr. Ananya Iyer
members.bio: Former social entrepreneur and tech policy researcher. Ananya has over 15 years of experience building tech-for-good initiatives and holds a Ph.D. in Community Informatics.

members.name: Vikram Rathore
members.bio: Operations veteran who spent a decade coordinating global scaling initiatives at top software firms before shifting full-time to build Project Sankalp's mentorship network.

members.name: Sarah D'Souza
members.bio: Systems engineer specializing in off-grid power and local-first networking. Sarah leads our smart lab setups, ensuring stable systems in solar-only rural areas.

members.name: Dinesh Kumar
members.bio: Experienced educator and full-stack software developer. Dinesh writes our responsive, accessible learning modules and oversees tech mentor training.

partners.title: Our Trusted Partners & Supporters
partners.subtitle: Enabling our vision through hardware, funding, and operational support.

list.name: EcoGrid Energy Systems
list.description: Provides solar panels and battery storage units for our rural Smart Labs.

list.name: Apex University
list.description: Collaborates on student mentorship and volunteer recruitment credits.

list.name: Veloce Technologies
list.description: Donates laptops, monitors, and networking hardware for our labs.

list.name: Impact Foundation
list.description: Funder of our Community Social Tech Fellowship program.

stories.title: First Digital Graduate

testimonials.quote: Project Sankalp gave me more than computer skills. It connected me to mentors who helped me polish my resume and secure a software engineering internship.
testimonials.author: Priya Sharma

testimonials.quote: Running solar-powered digital labs in off-grid rural schools seemed impossible until Sankalp's technical team came and installed self-reliant local network boxes.
testimonials.author: Principal Jacob Thomas
